targets.notes: Microsoft plans to achieve this goal by reducing Scope 3 emissions (market-based and management’s criteria) by more than half and by reducing Scope 1 and 2 (market-based) emissions to near zero by the middle of the decade through energy efficiency work and reaching 100% renewable energy by 2025; this carbon inventory is what is in scope of the carbon negative commitment.
targets.title: Carbon negative by 2030

targets.notes: By 2050 Microsoft will remove from the atmosphere an equivalent amount of all the carbon the company has emitted either directly or by its electricity consumption since being founded in 1975; this carbon inventory is what is in scope of the carbon negative commitment.
targets.title: Remove all historical emissions by 2050

targets.boundary: Global Scope 1, Scope 2 market-based, and Scope 3 business air travel emissions.
targets.notes: Microsoft’s target is to be carbon neutral every year since fiscal year 2013 by matching the emissions within the carbon neutrality boundary with an equivalent amount of carbon offsets; starting in FY23, values for Scope 3 business air travel emissions follow management’s criteria as reported under Category 6 – Business Travel with SAFc; values reflect Microsoft’s carbon neutrality at the time of reporting.
targets.title: Carbon neutral every year since FY2013
